records.ety: [OE. stowen, fr. stowe a place, AS. stow; cf. Icel. eldstōa fireplace, hearth, OFries. stō, and E. stand. √163.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Stowed (stōd); p. pr. & vb. n. Stowing.]

defs: To place or arrange in a compact mass; to put in its proper place, or in a suitable place; to pack; as, to stowbags, bales, or casks in a ship's hold; to stow hay in a mow; to stow sheaves.
quotes: Some stow their oars, or stop the leaky sides.

defs: To put away in some place; to hide; to lodge.
quotes: Foul thief! where hast thou stowed my daughter?

defs: To arrange anything compactly in; to fill, by packing closely; as, to stow a box, car, or the hold of a ship.
